     .and_then(|m| m.get(&s))\n88\t                .cloned()\n89\t                .unwrap_or_default();\n90\t            for doc in &old_docs {\n91\t                if !new_docs.contains(doc.as_str()) {\n92\t                    lost.push(doc.clone());\n93\t                }\n94\t            }\n95\t        }\n96\t        lost\n97\t    }\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\tstruct RecordedWrite {\n101\t    doc_id: String,\n102\t    shard: ShardId,\n103\t    succeeded_on_old: bool,\n104\t    succeeded_on_new: bool,\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\t/// Simulate a dual-write. Returns what happened on each node.\n108\tfn dual_write(\n109\t    cluster: &mut SimCluster,\n110\t    old_node: &NodeId,\n111\t    new_node: &NodeId,\n112\t    shard: ShardId,\n113\t    doc_id: &str,\n114\t    old_fails: bool,\n115\t    new_fails: bool,\n116\t) -> RecordedWrite {\n117\t    if !old_fails {\n118\t        cluster.put(old_node, shard, doc_id);\n119\t    }\n120\t    if !new_fails {\n121\t        cluster.put(new_node, shard, doc_id);\n122\t    }\n123\t    RecordedWrite {\n124\t        doc_id: doc_id.to_string(),\n125\t        shard,\n126\t        succeeded_on_old: !old_fails,\n127\t        succeeded_on_new: !new_fails,\n128\t    }\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\t/// Build an InFlightWrite from a RecordedWrite.\n132\t/// Critically: if a node did NOT accept the write, mark it as *failed* (not\n133\t/// just missing from completed_nodes). This is what the drain logic expects —\n134\t/// `is_drained()` requires completed + failed == target count.\n135\tfn make_in_flight(w: &RecordedWrite, old_node: &NodeId, new_node: &NodeId) -> InFlightWrite {\n136\t    let mut completed = HashSet::new();\n137\t    let mut failed = HashMap::new();\n138\t\n139\t    if w.succeeded_on_old {\n140\t        completed.insert(old_node.clone());\n141\t    } else {\n142\t        failed.insert(old_node.clone(), \"write failed\".into());\n143\t    }\n144\t    if w.succeeded_on_new {\n145\t        completed.insert(new_node.clone());\n146\t    } else {\n147\t        failed.insert(new_node.clone(), \"write failed\".into());\n148\t    }\n149\t\n150\t    InFlightWrite {\n151\t        doc_id: w.doc_id.clone(),\n152\t        shard: w.shard,\n153\t        target_nodes: vec![old_node.clone(), new_node.clone()],\n154\t        completed_nodes: completed,\n155\t        failed_nodes: failed,\n156\t        submitted_at: Instant::now(),\n157\t    }\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\t/// Run a full delta pass on the simulated cluster: copy every doc on old but\n161\t/// not on new to new, then call shard_delta_complete for each shard.\n162\tfn run_delta_pass(\n163\t    coord: &mut MigrationCoordinator,\n164\t    cluster: &mut SimCluster,\n165\t    mid: miroir_core::migration::MigrationId,\n166\t    old_node: &NodeId,\n167\t    new_node: &NodeId,\n168\t    shards: &[ShardId],\n169\t) {\n170\t    for &s in shards {\n171\t        let lost: Vec<String> = cluster\n172\t            .data\n173\t            .get(old_node)\n174\t            .and_then(|m| m.get(&s))\n175\t            .map(|docs| {\n176\t                docs.iter()\n177\t                    .filter(|d| {\n178\t                        !cluster\n179\t                            .data\n180\t                            .get(new_node)\n181\t                            .and_then(|m| m.get(&s))\n182\t                            .is_some_and(|nd| nd.contains(*d))\n183\t                    })\n184\t                    .cloned()\n185\t                    .collect()\n186\t            })\n187\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n188\t\n189\t        for doc_id in &lost {\n190\t            cluster.put(new_node, s, doc_id);\n191\t        }\n192\t        coord\n193\t            .shard_delta_complete(mid, s, lost.len() as u64)\n194\t            .unwrap();\n195\t    }\n196\t}\n197\t\n198\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n199\t// Test 1: AE on + delta pass on → 0 loss\n200\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n201\t\n202\t#[test]\n203\tfn cutover_chaos_with_anti_entropy() {\n204\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n205\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n206\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n207\t        ..Default::default()\n208\t    };\n209\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n210\t\n211\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n212\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n213\t    let shards = vec![shard(0), shard(1), shard(2)];\n214\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n215\t\n216\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n217\t\n218\t    // Pre-populate 1000 docs\n219\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n220\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n221\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n222\t    }\n223\t\n224\t    // Migration\n225\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n226\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n227\t\n228\t    // Dual-write phase: 2% failure on new\n229\t    let mut boundary: Vec<RecordedWrite> = Vec::new();\n230\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n231\t        let doc_id = format!(\"dw-{i}\");\n232\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n233\t        let new_fails = i % 50 == 0;\n234\t        let w = dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, new_fails);\n235\t        boundary.push(w);\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    // Background migration done\n239\t    for &s in &shards {\n240\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, s, 500).unwrap();\n241\t    }\n242\t\n243\t    // Boundary writes (arrive between CutoverBegin and begin_cutover)\n244\t    for i in 0..100u64 {\n245\t        let doc_id = format!(\"bnd-{i}\");\n246\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n247\t        let w = dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, false);\n248\t        boundary.push(w);\n249\t    }\n250\t\n251\t    // Register all writes as in-flight (drain will verify they've settled)\n252\t    for w in &boundary {\n253\t        coord.register_in_flight(make_in_flight(w, &old, &new));\n254\t    }\n255\t\n256\t    // Cutover\n257\t    coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n258\t    let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n259\t\n260\t    // Delta pass should be triggered (some writes failed on new)\n261\t    assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass);\n262\t\n263\t    // Verify there ARE lost docs before delta pass\n264\t    let lost_before = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n265\t    assert!(\n266\t        !lost_before.is_empty(),\n267\t        \"Expected some lost docs before delta pass\"\n268\t    );\n269\t\n270\t    // Delta pass repairs them\n271\t    run_delta_pass(&mut coord, &mut cluster, mid, &old, &new, &shards);\n272\t\n273\t    assert_eq!(\n274\t        coord.get_state(mid).unwrap().phase,\n275\t        MigrationPhase::CutoverCleanup\n276\t    );\n277\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n278\t\n279\t    // Final: 0 loss\n280\t    let lost = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n281\t    assert_eq!(\n282\t        lost.len(),\n283\t        0,\n284\t        \"Lost {} docs with AE on + delta pass\",\n285\t        lost.len()\n286\t    );\n287\t\n288\t    let all = cluster.all_docs_for_shards(&shards);\n289\t    assert_eq!(all.len(), 2100, \"Expected 2100 docs, got {}\", all.len());\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n293\t// Test 2: AE on + delta skipped → measurable loss (AE would repair later)\n294\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n295\t\n296\t#[test]\n297\tfn cutover_chaos_skip_delta_with_ae() {\n298\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n299\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n300\t        skip_delta_pass: true,\n301\t        ..Default::default()\n302\t    };\n303\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n304\t\n305\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n306\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n307\t    let shards = vec![shard(0), shard(1)];\n308\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n309\t\n310\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n311\t\n312\t    for i in 0..500u64 {\n313\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n314\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n315\t    }\n316\t\n317\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n318\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n319\t\n320\t    // Dual-write: 5% failure on new\n321\t    let mut expected_lost: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n322\t    let mut writes: Vec<RecordedWrite> = Vec::new();\n323\t    for i in 0..200u64 {\n324\t        let doc_id = format!(\"dw-{i}\");\n325\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n326\t        let new_fails = i % 20 == 0;\n327\t        let w = dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, new_fails);\n328\t        if w.succeeded_on_old && !w.succeeded_on_new {\n329\t            expected_lost.push(doc_id);\n330\t        }\n331\t        writes.push(w);\n332\t    }\n333\t\n334\t    // Boundary writes — all succeed\n335\t    for i in 0..50u64 {\n336\t        let doc_id = format!(\"bnd-{i}\");\n337\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n338\t        let w = dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, false);\n339\t        writes.push(w);\n340\t    }\n341\t\n342\t    for &s in &shards {\n343\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, s, 300).unwrap();\n344\t    }\n345\t\n346\t    // Register writes so drain can complete\n347\t    for w in &writes {\n348\t        coord.register_in_flight(make_in_flight(w, &old, &new));\n349\t    }\n350\t\n351\t    coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n352\t    let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n353\t    // skip_delta_pass → straight to cleanup\n354\t    assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverCleanup);\n355\t\n356\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n357\t\n358\t    // Measure loss — pre-existing docs (500) are on old only since background\n359\t    // migration copies them but our simulation doesn't track that copy. Plus the\n360\t    // dual-write failures. All would be repaired by AE in production.\n361\t    let lost = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n362\t    // Verify dual-write failures are a subset of lost docs\n363\t    for doc in &expected_lost {\n364\t        assert!(lost.contains(doc), \"Expected {doc} in lost set\");\n365\t    }\n366\t    assert!(\n367\t        !lost.is_empty(),\n368\t        \"Expected some lost docs when skipping delta pass\"\n369\t    );\n370\t\n371\t    eprintln!(\n372\t        \"\\n=== Skip Delta + AE ON ===\\n\\\n373\t         Dual-write failures (subset of lost): {}\\n\\\n374\t         Total docs lost after cutover (no delta pass): {}\\n\\\n375\t         All {} would be repaired by anti-entropy on next pass.\\n\",\n376\t        expected_lost.len(),\n377\t        lost.len(),\n378\t        lost.len()\n379\t    );\n380\t}\n381\t\n382\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n383\t// Test 3: AE off + delta pass on → 0 loss (delta pass is sufficient alone)\n384\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n385\t\n386\t#[test]\n387\tfn cutover_chaos_no_ae_with_delta() {\n388\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n389\t        anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n390\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n391\t        ..Default::default()\n392\t    };\n393\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n394\t\n395\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n396\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n397\t    let shards = vec![shard(0), shard(1), shard(2)];\n398\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n399\t\n400\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n401\t\n402\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n403\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n404\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n405\t    }\n406\t\n407\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n408\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n409\t\n410\t    // 2% failure on new\n411\t    let mut writes: Vec<RecordedWrite> = Vec::new();\n412\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n413\t        let doc_id = format!(\"dw-{i}\");\n414\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n415\t        let new_fails = i % 50 == 0;\n416\t        let w = dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, new_fails);\n417\t        writes.push(w);\n418\t    }\n419\t\n420\t    // Boundary: 1% failure\n421\t    for i in 0..200u64 {\n422\t        let doc_id = format!(\"bnd-{i}\");\n423\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n424\t        let new_fails = i % 100 == 0;\n425\t        let w = dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, new_fails);\n426\t        writes.push(w);\n427\t    }\n428\t\n429\t    for &s in &shards {\n430\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, s, 500).unwrap();\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    for w in &writes {\n434\t        coord.register_in_flight(make_in_flight(w, &old, &new));\n435\t    }\n436\t\n437\t    coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n438\t    let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n439\t    assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass);\n440\t\n441\t    run_delta_pass(&mut coord, &mut cluster, mid, &old, &new, &shards);\n442\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n443\t\n444\t    let lost = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n445\t    assert_eq!(\n446\t        lost.len(),\n447\t        0,\n448\t        \"Delta pass alone should provide 0 loss. Lost: {:?}\",\n449\t        &lost[..lost.len().min(10)]\n450\t    );\n451\t}\n452\t\n453\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n454\t// Test 4: AE off + delta skipped → refused at config validation\n455\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n456\t\n457\t#[test]\n458\tfn cutover_chaos_no_ae_no_delta_blocked() {\n459\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n460\t        anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n461\t        skip_delta_pass: true,\n462\t        ..Default::default()\n463\t    };\n464\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n465\t\n466\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = [(shard(0), node(\"old-0\"))].into_iter().collect();\n467\t\n468\t    let result = coord.begin_migration(node(\"new-3\"), 0, affected);\n469\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n470\t    assert!(matches!(\n471\t        result.unwrap_err(),\n472\t        MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy\n473\t    ));\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n477\t// Test 5: boundary burst — writes at every phase transition\n478\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n479\t\n480\t#[test]\n481\tfn cutover_chaos_boundary_burst() {\n482\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n483\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n484\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n485\t        ..Default::default()\n486\t    };\n487\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n488\t\n489\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n490\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n491\t    let shards = vec![shard(0), shard(1)];\n492\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n493\t\n494\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n495\t\n496\t    // Pre-populate\n497\t    for i in 0..500u64 {\n498\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n499\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n500\t    }\n501\t\n502\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n503\t\n504\t    let mut all_writes: Vec<RecordedWrite> = Vec::new();\n505\t\n506\t    // Burst 1: ComputingAssignments → DualWriteMigrating\n507\t    for i in 0..50u64 {\n508\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n509\t        let w = dual_write(\n510\t            &mut cluster,\n511\t            &old,\n512\t            &new,\n513\t            s,\n514\t            &format!(\"b1-{i}\"),\n515\t            false,\n516\t            false,\n517\t        );\n518\t        all_writes.push(w);\n519\t    }\n520\t\n521\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n522\t\n523\t    // Burst 2: during DualWriteMigrating, some fail on new\n524\t    for i in 0..100u64 {\n525\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n526\t        let new_fails = i % 25 == 0;\n527\t        let w = dual_write(\n528\t            &mut cluster,\n529\t            &old,\n530\t            &new,\n531\t            s,\n532\t            &format!(\"b2-{i}\"),\n533\t            false,\n534\t            new_fails,\n535\t        );\n536\t        all_writes.push(w);\n537\t    }\n538\t\n539\t    // Burst 3: just before each shard_migration_complete\n540\t    for &s in &shards {\n541\t        let w = dual_write(\n542\t            &mut cluster,\n543\t            &old,\n544\t            &new,\n545\t            s,\n546\t            &format!(\"b3-{s:?}\"),\n547\t            false,\n548\t            false,\n549\t        );\n550\t        all_writes.push(w);\n551\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, s, 300).unwrap();\n552\t    }\n553\t\n554\t    // Burst 4: CutoverBegin → begin_cutover\n555\t    for i in 0..50u64 {\n556\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n557\t        let w = dual_write(\n558\t            &mut cluster,\n559\t            &old,\n560\t            &new,\n561\t            s,\n562\t            &format!(\"b4-{i}\"),\n563\t            false,\n564\t            false,\n565\t        );\n566\t        all_writes.push(w);\n567\t    }\n568\t\n569\t    // Register all completed writes for drain\n570\t    for w in &all_writes {\n571\t        coord.register_in_flight(make_in_flight(w, &old, &new));\n572\t    }\n573\t\n574\t    coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n575\t\n576\t    // Burst 5: during CutoverDraining — these go to old only\n577\t    // In production, the delta pass catches these. We register them as\n578\t    // failed on new so drain completes.\n579\t    for i in 0..50u64 {\n580\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n581\t        let doc_id = format!(\"b5-{i}\");\n582\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &doc_id);\n583\t        // Not on new — will be caught by delta pass\n584\t        let w = RecordedWrite {\n585\t            doc_id: doc_id.clone(),\n586\t            shard: s,\n587\t            succeeded_on_old: true,\n588\t            succeeded_on_new: false,\n589\t        };\n590\t        coord.register_in_flight(make_in_flight(&w, &old, &new));\n591\t        all_writes.push(w);\n592\t    }\n593\t\n594\t    let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n595\t    assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass);\n596\t\n597\t    run_delta_pass(&mut coord, &mut cluster, mid, &old, &new, &shards);\n598\t\n599\t    assert_eq!(\n600\t        coord.get_state(mid).unwrap().phase,\n601\t        MigrationPhase::CutoverCleanup\n602\t    );\n603\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n604\t\n605\t    let lost = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n606\t    assert_eq!(lost.len(), 0, \"Boundary burst lost {} docs\", lost.len());\n607\t\n608\t    let all = cluster.all_docs_for_shards(&shards);\n609\t    // 500 pre + 50 b1 + 100 b2 + 2 b3 + 50 b4 + 50 b5 = 752\n610\t    assert!(all.len() >= 750, \"Expected >= 750 docs, got {}\", all.len());\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n614\t// Test 6: high volume — 100K writes, measure loss rate with AE + delta\n615\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n616\t\n617\t#[test]\n618\tfn cutover_chaos_high_volume() {\n619\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n620\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n621\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n622\t        ..Default::default()\n623\t    };\n624\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n625\t\n626\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n627\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n628\t    let shards = vec![shard(0)];\n629\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n630\t\n631\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n632\t\n633\t    const TOTAL: u64 = 100_000;\n634\t\n635\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n636\t        cluster.put(&old, shards[0], &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n637\t    }\n638\t\n639\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n640\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n641\t\n642\t    // 1% failure on new, but we don't register as in-flight for perf.\n643\t    // We register a representative sample to drive delta pass.\n644\t    for i in 0..TOTAL {\n645\t        let doc_id = format!(\"w-{i}\");\n646\t        let new_fails = i % 100 == 0;\n647\t        dual_write(\n648\t            &mut cluster,\n649\t            &old,\n650\t            &new,\n651\t            shards[0],\n652\t            &doc_id,\n653\t            false,\n654\t            new_fails,\n655\t        );\n656\t    }\n657\t\n658\t    coord\n659\t        .shard_migration_complete(mid, shards[0], 1000)\n660\t        .unwrap();\n661\t\n662\t    // Boundary writes\n663\t    for i in 0..100u64 {\n664\t        dual_write(\n665\t            &mut cluster,\n666\t            &old,\n667\t            &new,\n668\t            shards[0],\n669\t            &format!(\"bnd-{i}\"),\n670\t            false,\n671\t            false,\n672\t        );\n673\t    }\n674\t\n675\t    // Register one failed write to force delta pass\n676\t    coord.register_in_flight(InFlightWrite {\n677\t        doc_id: \"w-0\".into(),\n678\t        shard: shards[0],\n679\t        target_nodes: vec![old.clone(), new.clone()],\n680\t        completed_nodes: HashSet::from([old.clone()]),\n681\t        failed_nodes: HashMap::from([(new.clone(), \"simulated failure\".into())]),\n682\t        submitted_at: Instant::now(),\n683\t    });\n684\t\n685\t    coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n686\t    let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n687\t    assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass);\n688\t\n689\t    // Delta pass\n690\t    let lost: Vec<String> = cluster\n691\t        .data\n692\t        .get(&old)\n693\t        .and_then(|m| m.get(&shards[0]))\n694\t        .map(|docs| {\n695\t            docs.iter()\n696\t                .filter(|d| {\n697\t                    !cluster\n698\t                        .data\n699\t                        .get(&new)\n700\t                        .and_then(|m| m.get(&shards[0]))\n701\t                        .is_some_and(|nd| nd.contains(*d))\n702\t                })\n703\t                .cloned()\n704\t                .collect()\n705\t        })\n706\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n707\t\n708\t    let delta_count = lost.len();\n709\t    for doc_id in &lost {\n710\t        cluster.put(&new, shards[0], doc_id);\n711\t    }\n712\t    coord\n713\t        .shard_delta_complete(mid, shards[0], delta_count as u64)\n714\t        .unwrap();\n715\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n716\t\n717\t    let lost_after = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n718\t    assert_eq!(\n719\t        lost_after.len(),\n720\t        0,\n721\t        \"High-volume: lost {}/{} writes ({}%)\",\n722\t        lost_after.len(),\n723\t        TOTAL,\n724\t        (lost_after.len() as f64 / TOTAL as f64) * 100.0\n725\t    );\n726\t\n727\t    let all = cluster.all_docs_for_shards(&shards);\n728\t    let expected = 1000 + TOTAL as usize + 100;\n729\t    assert_eq!(all.len(), expected);\n730\t\n731\t    eprintln!(\n732\t        \"\\n=== High Volume ({}K writes) ===\\n\\\n733\t         Writes failed on new during dual-write: {}\\n\\\n734\t         Caught by delta pass: {}\\n\\\n735\t         Lost after delta pass: 0\\n\\\n736\t         Loss rate: 0/{TOTAL} (0.000%)\\n\",\n737\t        TOTAL / 1000,\n738\t        TOTAL / 100,\n739\t        delta_count,\n740\t    );\n741\t}\n742\t\n743\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n744\t// Test 7: AE off + delta on, 50K writes — confirm 0 loss rate\n745\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n746\t\n747\t#[test]\n748\tfn cutover_chaos_loss_rate_no_ae_delta() {\n749\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n750\t        anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n751\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n752\t        ..Default::default()\n753\t    };\n754\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n755\t\n756\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n757\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n758\t    let shards = vec![shard(0), shard(1)];\n759\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n760\t\n761\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n762\t\n763\t    const TOTAL: u64 = 50_000;\n764\t\n765\t    for i in 0..500u64 {\n766\t        cluster.put(&old, shards[i as usize % shards.len()], &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n767\t    }\n768\t\n769\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n770\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n771\t\n772\t    let mut failure_count = 0u64;\n773\t    for i in 0..TOTAL {\n774\t        let doc_id = format!(\"w-{i}\");\n775\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n776\t        let new_fails = i % 100 == 0;\n777\t        if new_fails {\n778\t            failure_count += 1;\n779\t        }\n780\t        dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, new_fails);\n781\t    }\n782\t\n783\t    for &s in &shards {\n784\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, s, 300).unwrap();\n785\t    }\n786\t\n787\t    // Register one failed write to force delta pass\n788\t    coord.register_in_flight(InFlightWrite {\n789\t        doc_id: \"w-0\".into(),\n790\t        shard: shards[0],\n791\t        target_nodes: vec![old.clone(), new.clone()],\n792\t        completed_nodes: HashSet::from([old.clone()]),\n793\t        failed_nodes: HashMap::from([(new.clone(), \"simulated failure\".into())]),\n794\t        submitted_at: Instant::now(),\n795\t    });\n796\t\n797\t    coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n798\t    let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n799\t    assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass);\n800\t\n801\t    run_delta_pass(&mut coord, &mut cluster, mid, &old, &new, &shards);\n802\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n803\t\n804\t    let lost = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n805\t    assert_eq!(\n806\t        lost.len(),\n807\t        0,\n808\t        \"Lost {} docs without AE but with delta pass\",\n809\t        lost.len()\n810\t    );\n811\t\n812\t    eprintln!(\n813\t        \"\\n=== Loss Rate: AE OFF + Delta Pass ON ===\\n\\\n814\t         Total writes: {TOTAL}\\n\\\n815\t         Writes failed on new during dual-write: {failure_count}\\n\\\n816\t         Docs lost after delta pass: 0\\n\\\n817\t         Loss rate: 0/{TOTAL} (0.000%)\\n\\\n818\t         Conclusion: Delta pass alone provides 0-loss cutover.\\n\"\n819\t    );\n820\t}\n821\t\n822\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n823\t// Test 8: validation gates block unsafe configuration\n824\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n825\t\n826\t#[test]\n827\tfn cutover_chaos_validation_gates() {\n828\t    use miroir_core::anti_entropy::{validate_migration_safety, AntiEntropyConfig};\n829\t\n830\t    // Gate 1: MigrationCoordinator refuses unsafe config\n831\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n832\t        anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n833\t        skip_delta_pass: true,\n834\t        ..Default::default()\n835\t    };\n836\t    let coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n837\t    assert!(coord.validate_safety().is_err());\n838\t\n839\t    // Gate 2: Cross-module anti_entropy validation\n840\t    let ae = AntiEntropyConfig {\n841\t        enabled: false,\n842\t        ..Default::default()\n843\t    };\n844\t    let mc = MigrationConfig {\n845\t        skip_delta_pass: true,\n846\t        anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n847\t        ..Default::default()\n848\t    };\n849\t    assert!(validate_migration_safety(&ae, &mc).is_err());\n850\t\n851\t    // Gate 3: Warning when AE disabled\n852\t    use miroir_core::anti_entropy::migration_warning_if_ae_disabled;\n853\t    assert!(migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(false).is_some());\n854\t    assert!(migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(true).is_none());\n855\t\n856\t    // Safe configs should pass\n857\t    let safe_config = MigrationConfig {\n858\t        anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n859\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n860\t        ..Default::default()\n861\t    };\n862\t    let safe_coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(safe_config);\n863\t    assert!(safe_coord.validate_safety().is_ok());\n864\t\n865\t    let safe_ae = AntiEntropyConfig {\n866\t        enabled: true,\n867\t        ..Default::default()\n868\t    };\n869\t    let safe_mc = MigrationConfig {\n870\t        skip_delta_pass: true,\n871\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n872\t        ..Default::default()\n873\t    };\n874\t    assert!(validate_migration_safety(&safe_ae, &safe_mc).is_ok());\n875\t}\n876\t\n877\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n878\t// Test 9: tight-loop boundary — writes at exact cutover transition instant\n879\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n880\t//\n881\t// Simulates docs arriving at the instant of `active` transition (step 7 in\n882\t// plan §2 \"Adding a node\"). The dangerous window is between CutoverBegin and\n883\t// CutoverCleanup. Writes that succeed on OLD but fail on NEW during this\n884\t// window MUST be caught by the delta pass.\n885\t//\n886\t// This variant drives writes at every single state transition boundary with\n887\t// deterministic 5% failure injection on the new node.\n888\t\n889\t#[test]\n890\tfn cutover_chaos_tight_loop_boundary() {\n891\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n892\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n893\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n894\t        ..Default::default()\n895\t    };\n896\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n897\t\n898\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n899\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n900\t    let shards = vec![shard(0), shard(1), shard(2)];\n901\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n902\t\n903\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n904\t\n905\t    // Pre-populate 1000 docs\n906\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n907\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n908\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n909\t    }\n910\t\n911\t    let mut all_writes: Vec<RecordedWrite> = Vec::new();\n912\t\n913\t    // Phase: ComputingAssignments → begin_migration\n914\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n915\t\n916\t    // Burst: writes BEFORE dual-write starts (should go to old only in prod,\n917\t    // but we simulate them as dual-write for boundary testing)\n918\t    for i in 0..200u64 {\n919\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n920\t        let new_fails = i % 20 == 0; // 5% failure\n921\t        let w = dual_write(\n922\t            &mut cluster,\n923\t            &old,\n924\t            &new,\n925\t            s,\n926\t            &format!(\"t0-{i}\"),\n927\t            false,\n928\t            new_fails,\n929\t        );\n930\t        all_writes.push(w);\n931\t    }\n932\t\n933\t    // Transition: ComputingAssignments → DualWriteMigrating\n934\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n935\t\n936\t    // Burst: writes during active dual-write migration\n937\t    for i in 0..500u64 {\n938\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n939\t        let new_fails = i % 20 == 0; // 5% failure\n940\t        let w = dual_write(\n941\t            &mut cluster,\n942\t            &old,\n943\t            &new,\n944\t            s,\n945\t            &format!(\"t1-{i}\"),\n946\t            false,\n947\t            new_fails,\n948\t        );\n949\t        all_writes.push(w);\n950\t    }\n951\t\n952\t    // Transition: complete shard migrations one at a time, writing between each\n953\t    for (idx, &s) in shards.iter().enumerate() {\n954\t        // Writes between shard completions\n955\t        for j in 0..50u64 {\n956\t            let s2 = shards[j as usize % shards.len()];\n957\t            let new_fails = j % 20 == 0;\n958\t            let w = dual_write(\n959\t                &mut cluster,\n960\t                &old,\n961\t                &new,\n962\t                s2,\n963\t                &format!(\"t2-{idx}-{j}\"),\n964\t                false,\n965\t                new_fails,\n966\t            );\n967\t            all_writes.push(w);\n968\t        }\n969\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, s, 500).unwrap();\n970\t    }\n971\t\n972\t    // Transition: CutoverBegin → begin_cutover\n973\t    // Rapid-fire writes at the exact boundary\n974\t    for i in 0..300u64 {\n975\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n976\t        let new_fails = i % 20 == 0;\n977\t        let w = dual_write(\n978\t            &mut cluster,\n979\t            &old,\n980\t            &new,\n981\t            s,\n982\t            &format!(\"t3-{i}\"),\n983\t            false,\n984\t            new_fails,\n985\t        );\n986\t        all_writes.push(w);\n987\t    }\n988\t\n989\t    // Register all writes for drain tracking\n990\t    for w in &all_writes {\n991\t        coord.register_in_flight(make_in_flight(w, &old, &new));\n992\t    }\n993\t\n994\t    // Cutover: stop dual-write, drain in-flight\n995\t    coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n996\t\n997\t    // Writes during draining — these go to old only\n998\t    for i in 0..200u64 {\n999\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n1000\t        let doc_id = format!(\"t4-{i}\");\n1001\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &doc_id);\n1002\t        let w = RecordedWrite {\n1003\t            doc_id: doc_id.clone(),\n1004\t            shard: s,\n1005\t            succeeded_on_old: true,\n1006\t            succeeded_on_new: false,\n1007\t        };\n1008\t        coord.register_in_flight(make_in_flight(&w, &old, &new));\n1009\t        all_writes.push(w);\n1010\t    }\n1011\t\n1012\t    let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n1013\t    assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass);\n1014\t\n1015\t    // Delta pass catches everything\n1016\t    run_delta_pass(&mut coord, &mut cluster, mid, &old, &new, &shards);\n1017\t\n1018\t    assert_eq!(\n1019\t        coord.get_state(mid).unwrap().phase,\n1020\t        MigrationPhase::CutoverCleanup\n1021\t    );\n1022\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n1023\t\n1024\t    // Assert: 0 lost docs\n1025\t    let lost = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n1026\t    assert_eq!(\n1027\t        lost.len(),\n1028\t        0,\n1029\t        \"Tight-loop boundary test lost {} docs\",\n1030\t        lost.len()\n1031\t    );\n1032\t\n1033\t    let all = cluster.all_docs_for_shards(&shards);\n1034\t    // 1000 pre + 200 t0 + 500 t1 + 150 t2 + 300 t3 + 200 t4 = 2350\n1035\t    assert!(\n1036\t        all.len() >= 2350,\n1037\t        \"Expected >= 2350 docs, got {}\",\n1038\t        all.len()\n1039\t    );\n1040\t\n1041\t    eprintln!(\n1042\t        \"\\n=== Tight-Loop Boundary ===\\n\\\n1043\t         Total writes at boundaries: {}\\n\\\n1044\t         Docs lost after delta pass: 0\\n\\\n1045\t         Loss rate: 0/{} (0.000%)\\n\",\n1046\t        all_writes.len(),\n1047\t        all_writes.len()\n1048\t    );\n1049\t}\n1050\t\n1051\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1052\t// Test 10: 1M write loss rate measurement — AE on + delta on\n1053\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1054\t//\n1055\t// Acceptance criterion: loss rate < 1 per 1M writes.\n1056\t\n1057\t#[test]\n1058\tfn cutover_chaos_loss_rate_1m_ae_on() {\n1059\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n1060\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n1061\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n1062\t        ..Default::default()\n1063\t    };\n1064\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n1065\t\n1066\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n1067\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n1068\t    let shards = vec![shard(0)];\n1069\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n1070\t\n1071\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n1072\t\n1073\t    const TOTAL: u64 = 1_000_000;\n1074\t    const FAIL_RATE: u64 = 100; // 1% failure on new\n1075\t\n1076\t    // Pre-populate\n1077\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n1078\t        cluster.put(&old, shards[0], &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n1079\t    }\n1080\t\n1081\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n1082\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n1083\t\n1084\t    // 1M writes with 1% deterministic failure on new\n1085\t    let mut failure_count = 0u64;\n1086\t    for i in 0..TOTAL {\n1087\t        let doc_id = format!(\"w-{i}\");\n1088\t        let new_fails = i % FAIL_RATE == 0;\n1089\t        if new_fails {\n1090\t            failure_count += 1;\n1091\t        }\n1092\t        dual_write(\n1093\t            &mut cluster,\n1094\t            &old,\n1095\t            &new,\n1096\t            shards[0],\n1097\t            &doc_id,\n1098\t            false,\n1099\t            new_fails,\n1100\t        );\n1101\t    }\n1102\t\n1103\t    coord\n1104\t        .shard_migration_complete(mid, shards[0], 1000)\n1105\t        .unwrap();\n1106\t\n1107\t    // Boundary writes\n1108\t    for i in 0..500u64 {\n1109\t        dual_write(\n1110\t            &mut cluster,\n1111\t            &old,\n1112\t            &new,\n1113\t            shards[0],\n1114\t            &format!(\"bnd-{i}\"),\n1115\t            false,\n1116\t            false,\n1117\t        );\n1118\t    }\n1119\t\n1120\t    // Register one known-failed write to force delta pass\n1121\t    coord.register_in_flight(InFlightWrite {\n1122\t        doc_id: \"w-0\".into(),\n1123\t        shard: shards[0],\n1124\t        target_nodes: vec![old.clone(), new.clone()],\n1125\t        completed_nodes: HashSet::from([old.clone()]),\n1126\t        failed_nodes: HashMap::from([(new.clone(), \"simulated failure\".into())]),\n1127\t        submitted_at: Instant::now(),\n1128\t    });\n1129\t\n1130\t    coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n1131\t    let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n1132\t    assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass);\n1133\t\n1134\t    // Delta pass\n1135\t    let lost: Vec<String> = cluster\n1136\t        .data\n1137\t        .get(&old)\n1138\t        .and_then(|m| m.get(&shards[0]))\n1139\t        .map(|docs| {\n1140\t            docs.iter()\n1141\t                .filter(|d| {\n1142\t                    !cluster\n1143\t                        .data\n1144\t                        .get(&new)\n1145\t                        .and_then(|m| m.get(&shards[0]))\n1146\t                        .is_some_and(|nd| nd.contains(*d))\n1147\t                })\n1148\t                .cloned()\n1149\t                .collect()\n1150\t        })\n1151\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n1152\t\n1153\t    let delta_count = lost.len();\n1154\t    for doc_id in &lost {\n1155\t        cluster.put(&new, shards[0], doc_id);\n1156\t    }\n1157\t    coord\n1158\t        .shard_delta_complete(mid, shards[0], delta_count as u64)\n1159\t        .unwrap();\n1160\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n1161\t\n1162\t    let lost_after = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n1163\t    let loss_rate = lost_after.len() as f64 / TOTAL as f64;\n1164\t\n1165\t    assert_eq!(\n1166\t        lost_after.len(),\n1167\t        0,\n1168\t        \"1M write test: lost {}/{} writes ({:.6}%) — must be 0\",\n1169\t        lost_after.len(),\n1170\t        TOTAL,\n1171\t        loss_rate * 100.0\n1172\t    );\n1173\t\n1174\t    eprintln!(\n1175\t        \"\\n=== 1M Write Loss Rate: AE ON + Delta Pass ON ===\\n\\\n1176\t         Total writes: {TOTAL}\\n\\\n1177\t         Deterministic failures on new: {failure_count}\\n\\\n1178\t         Caught by delta pass: {delta_count}\\n\\\n1179\t         Lost after cutover: 0\\n\\\n1180\t         Loss rate: 0/{TOTAL} (0.000%)\\n\\\n1181\t         PASS: < 1 per 1M writes\\n\"\n1182\t    );\n1183\t}\n1184\t\n1185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1186\t// Test 11: AE off + delta off → quantify loss rate (NOT started, refused)\n1187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1188\t//\n1189\t// This configuration is refused by the MigrationCoordinator. We bypass the\n1190\t// coordinator to measure what WOULD happen — the loss rate justifies the\n1191\t// hard refusal policy.\n1192\t\n1193\t#[test]\n1194\tfn cutover_chaos_loss_rate_no_ae_no_delta() {\n1195\t    // This config is unsafe — the coordinator would refuse it.\n1196\t    // We construct a bare cluster to measure what would happen.\n1197\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n1198\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n1199\t    let shards = vec![shard(0)];\n1200\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n1201\t\n1202\t    const TOTAL: u64 = 100_000;\n1203\t    const FAIL_RATE: u64 = 50; // 2% failure on new\n1204\t\n1205\t    // Pre-populate\n1206\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n1207\t        cluster.put(&old, shards[0], &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n1208\t    }\n1209\t\n1210\t    // Simulate dual-write without delta pass or AE\n1211\t    let mut lost_count = 0u64;\n1212\t    for i in 0..TOTAL {\n1213\t        let doc_id = format!(\"w-{i}\");\n1214\t        let new_fails = i % FAIL_RATE == 0;\n1215\t        dual_write(\n1216\t            &mut cluster,\n1217\t            &old,\n1218\t            &new,\n1219\t            shards[0],\n1220\t            &doc_id,\n1221\t            false,\n1222\t            new_fails,\n1223\t        );\n1224\t        if new_fails {\n1225\t            lost_count += 1;\n1226\t        }\n1227\t    }\n1228\t\n1229\t    // Boundary writes (all succeed)\n1230\t    for i in 0..500u64 {\n1231\t        dual_write(\n1232\t            &mut cluster,\n1233\t            &old,\n1234\t            &new,\n1235\t            shards[0],\n1236\t            &format!(\"bnd-{i}\"),\n1237\t            false,\n1238\t            false,\n1239\t        );\n1240\t    }\n1241\t\n1242\t    // Measure what's on old but not on new\n1243\t    let lost = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n1244\t    let loss_rate = lost.len() as f64 / TOTAL as f64;\n1245\t\n1246\t    // Pre-existing docs (1000) are also lost since we skipped background\n1247\t    // migration. Focus on dual-write losses.\n1248\t    assert!(\n1249\t        lost_count > 0,\n1250\t        \"Expected measurable loss without delta pass\"\n1251\t    );\n1252\t    assert_eq!(\n1253\t        lost_count,\n1254\t        lost.len() as u64 - 1000, // subtract pre-existing\n1255\t        \"Dual-write losses don't match expected count\"\n1256\t    );\n1257\t\n1258\t    eprintln!(\n1259\t        \"\\n=== Loss Rate: AE OFF + Delta OFF (hypothetical) ===\\n\\\n1260\t         Total writes: {TOTAL}\\n\\\n1261\t         Dual-write failures (old ok, new failed): {lost_count}\\n\\\n1262\t         Total docs missing on new (incl. pre-existing): {}\\n\\\n1263\t         Dual-write loss rate: {}/{TOTAL} ({:.4}%)\\n\\\n1264\t         Decision: MigrationCoordinator REFUSES this configuration.\\n\\\n1265\t         Justification: {:.4}% loss rate is unacceptable.\\n\",\n1266\t        lost.len(),\n1267\t        lost_count,\n1268\t        loss_rate * 100.0,\n1269\t        loss_rate * 100.0\n1270\t    );\n1271\t\n1272\t    // Verify the coordinator does refuse\n1273\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n1274\t        anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n1275\t        skip_delta_pass: true,\n1276\t        ..Default::default()\n1277\t    };\n1278\t    let coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n1279\t    assert!(\n1280\t        coord.validate_safety().is_err(),\n1281\t        \"Coordinator must refuse unsafe config\"\n1282\t    );\n1283\t}\n1284\t\n1285\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1286\t// Test 12: concurrent writes during entire migration lifecycle\n1287\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1288\t//\n1289\t// Simulates a realistic workload: writes arriving continuously through the\n1290\t// entire migration lifecycle, including at the exact CutoverActivate and\n1291\t// CutoverCleanup transitions.\n1292\t\n1293\t#[test]\n1294\tfn cutover_chaos_concurrent_migration_writes() {\n1295\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n1296\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n1297\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n1298\t        ..Default::default()\n1299\t    };\n1300\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n1301\t\n1302\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n1303\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n1304\t    let shards = vec![shard(0), shard(1)];\n1305\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n1306\t\n1307\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n1308\t\n1309\t    // Pre-populate\n1310\t    for i in 0..500u64 {\n1311\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n1312\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n1313\t    }\n1314\t\n1315\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n1316\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n1317\t\n1318\t    // Continuous writes through dual-write phase\n1319\t    let mut writes: Vec<RecordedWrite> = Vec::new();\n1320\t    for i in 0..5000u64 {\n1321\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n1322\t        let new_fails = i % 50 == 0; // 2% failure\n1323\t        let w = dual_write(\n1324\t            &mut cluster,\n1325\t            &old,\n1326\t            &new,\n1327\t            s,\n1328\t            &format!(\"cw-{i}\"),\n1329\t            false,\n1330\t            new_fails,\n1331\t        );\n1332\t        writes.push(w);\n1333\t    }\n1334\t\n1335\t    // Complete shard migrations\n1336\t    for &s in &shards {\n1337\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, s, 300).unwrap();\n1338\t    }\n1339\t\n1340\t    // Writes between CutoverBegin and begin_cutover\n1341\t    for i in 0..500u64 {\n1342\t        let s = shards[i       s,\n1349\t            &format!(\"cb-{i}\"),\n1350\t            false,\n1351\t            new_fails,\n1352\t        );\n1353\t        writes.push(w);\n1354\t    }\n1355\t\n1356\t    // Register ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1410\t// Test 13: 3-node cluster cutover — matches task design exactly\n1411\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1412\t//\n1413\t// Task design:\n1414\t// 1. Start 3-node cluster, write 1000 docs\n1415\t// 2. Trigger node addition\n1416\t// 3. During dual-write, rapid-fire new writes\n1417\t// 4. rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n8\t///\n9\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n10\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n11\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n12\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n13\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n14\t    h.finish()\n15\t}\n16\t\n17\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n18\t///\n19\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n20\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n21\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n22\t        .iter()\n23\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n24\t        .collect();\n25\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| b.0.cmp(&a.0));\n26\t    scored\n27\t        .into_iter()\n28\t        .take(rf)\n29\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n30\t        .collect()\n31\t}\n32\t\n33\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n34\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n35\t    topology\n36\t        .groups()\n37\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n38\t        .collect()\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n42\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n43\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n44\t}\n45\t\n46\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n47\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n48\t    (0..shard_count)\n49\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n50\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n51\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n52\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n53\t        })\n54\t        .collect::<std::collections::HashSet<_>>()\n55\t        .into_iter()\n56\t        .collect()\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n60\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n61\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n62\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n63\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n64\t}\n65\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_02b80182e0b445e7bab3c58f","session_id":"b5c88f03-b150-4244-b7ac-1e69669e57e8","uuid":"c6722ec9-90a9-4c94-9ede-52451f5e03ed","timestamp":"2026-05-08T19:24:10.407Z"}

}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_02b80182e0b445e7bab3c58f","session_id":"b5c88f03-b150-4244-b7ac-1e69669e57e8","uuid":"9d9ecb37-088b-4b03-a3ac-3b9e8d0d8d55","timestamp":"2026-05-08T19:24:10.409Z"}

The plan states that this \"doubles transient storage and write load.\" This benchmark confirms that estimate using the actual routing code with synthetic corpora.\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/benchmarks/resharding-load.md-8-\ndocs/benchmarks/resharding-load.md-9-**Conclusion: Storage amplification is exactly 2.0× and dual-write amplification is exactly 2.0× across all tested scenarios.** However, peak write amplification (dual-write + backfill) depends heavily on the backfill throttle rate relative to the incoming write rate and can reach 500× or more if the backfill runs unthrottled against a low-write corpus.\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/benchmarks/resharding-load.md-10-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/benchmarks/resharding-load.md-11-## Test Matrix\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/benchmarks/resharding-load.md-12-\n--\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/trade-offs.md-1-# Miroir Trade-Offs and Design Decisions\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/trade-offs.md-2-\ndocs/trade-offs.md:3:## Shard Migration Write Safety (Plan §15 OP#1)\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/trade-offs.md-4-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/trade-offs.md-5-### Problem\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/trade-offs.md-6-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/trade-offs.md-7-During node addition, documents written at the exact cutover boundary can be\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/trade-offs.md-8-lost if they succeed on the OLD node but fail on the NEW node. The dangerous\n--\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-1-# P12.OP2: Lightweight Raft vs. Redis for Task State HA\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-2-\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-3-**Date:** 2026-04-18\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-4-**Status:** Decision recorded — revisit before v2.0, do not ship in v0.x or v1.0\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-5-**Bead:** miroir-zc2.2\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md:6:**Plan ref:** §15 Open Problem #2, §4 Task store schema, §14.2 Per-pod memory budget\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-7-**Prototype:** `crates/miroir-core/src/raft_proto/` (feature-gated behind `raft-proto`)\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-8-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-9----\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-10-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-11-## Executive Summary\n--\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-374-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-375-1. **Redis works.** It's the industry-standard solution for shared state across stateless replicas. The operational burden of running Redis is well-understood and can be delegated to managed services (ElastiCache, Upstash, Redis Cloud) if self-hosting is undesirable.\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-376-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-377-2. **The write latency penalty is material.** Miroir's task store writes happen on the critical path of document mutations. Adding 2–5 ms of consensus latency per write, when Redis adds <1 ms, is a measurable degradation that the decision gate explicitly forbids.\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-378-\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md:379:3. **The complexity budget is better spent elsewhere.** Miroir's v1.0 has 21 advanced capabilities to ship (§13.1–§13.21), each with its own correctness surface. Adding a Raft implementation to the v1.0 scope would be a significant distraction with high downside risk.\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-380-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-381-4. **Raft's advantage (no external dependency) is modest for K8s deployments.** In Kubernetes, Redis is a standard add-on (Helm subchart, Bitnami chart, or managed service). It is not a novel operational burden. The real benefit of eliminating Redis would be for single-node deployments — but those already use SQLite.\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-382-\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-383-5. **The read advantage is irrelevant for Miroir.** Sub-millisecond reads from Redis vs. sub-millisecond reads from local SQLite — the difference is invisible to clients and to the proxy's p99 latency budget.\n/home/coding/miroir/docs/research/raft-task-store.md-384-\n--\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-492-- LiteFS: https://github.com/superfly/litefs\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-493-- rrqlite (openraft + SQLite): https://github.com/yuyang0/rrqlite\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-494-- libSQL / Turso: https://github.com/tursodatabase/libsql\ndocs/research/raft-task-store.md-495-- Miroir plan §4 (task store schema): `docs/plan/plan.md` lines 531–766\n\n[Showing results with pagination = limit: 50]"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_02b80182e0b445e7bab3c58f","session_id":"b5c88f03-b150-4244-b7ac-1e69669e57e8","uuid":"e2f05ca5-3cd3-449a-afdd-270c2b895430","timestamp":"2026-05-08T19:24:13.931Z"}

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the miroir codebase. Let me create a detailed overview of the migration and sharding system.\n\n## Comprehensive Overview of Miroir Sharding and Migration System\n\nBased on my exploration of the miroir codebase, here's a detailed analysis of the shard migration system, dual-write cutover sequencing, and anti-entropy reconciler:\n\n---\n\n### **1. Shard Migration System Architecture**\n\n#### **Core Files:**\n\n**`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/migration.rs`** (817 lines)\n- **Key Types:**\n  - `MigrationCoordinator`: Main state machine for node-addition migrations\n  - `MigrationState`: Tracks overall migration progress\n  - `ShardMigrationState`: Per-shard migration state\n  - `MigrationPhase`: 8-phase cutover sequence\n  - `InFlightWrite`: Tracks write completion during drain phase\n\n- **Key Functions:**\n  - `begin_migration()`: Initiates node-addition migration with safety validation\n  - `begin_dual_write()`: Starts dual-write phase\n  - `begin_cutover()`: Initiates cutover sequence\n  - `complete_drain()`: Completes drain and transitions to delta pass or activation\n  - `shard_delta_complete()`: Records delta pass completion\n  - `activate_shards()`: Marks shards as active on new node\n\n**`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/reshard.rs`** (597 lines)\n- **Key Types:**\n  - `ReshardingConfig`: Configuration for resharding operations\n  - `TimeWindow`: UTC time window for scheduling restrictions\n  - `SimParams`: Parameters for load simulation\n  - `SimResult`: Results from simulation runs\n\n- **Key Functions:**\n  - `check_window()`: Validates resharding can run at current time\n  - `simulate()`: Runs resharding load simulation using actual routing code\n\n---\n\n### **2. Dual-Write Cutover Sequencing (Phase 4)**\n\n#### **Migration Phases (from `migration.rs`):**\n\n```rust\npub enum MigrationPhase {\n    ComputingAssignments,      // Computing which shards move to new node\n    DualWriteMigrating,        // Dual-write active; background migration in progress\n    CutoverBegin,             // Background migration done; beginning cutover\n    CutoverDraining,          // Stopping dual-write; waiting for in-flight writes\n    CutoverDeltaPass,         // Re-reading source to catch stragglers\n    CutoverActivate,          // Marking new node active; switching routing\n    CutoverCleanup,           // Deleting migrated shard data from old nodes\n    Complete,                 // All shards migrated; migration complete\n    Failed(String),           // Migration failed\n}\n```\n\n#### **Per-Shard States:**\n\n```rust\npub enum ShardMigrationState {\n    Pending,                          // Waiting for background migration\n    Migrating { docs_copied, pages_remaining },  // Active background pagination\n    MigrationComplete { docs_copied }, // Background migration complete\n    Draining { in_flight_count, docs_copied },  // In-flight writes draining\n    DeltaPass { docs_copied, delta_docs_copied },  // Re-reading source for stragglers\n    Active,                           // Node is active; old replica deleted\n    Failed { phase, reason },         // Migration failed\n}\n```\n\n#### **Cutover Sequence (The Critical Path):**\n\n**Phase 4 - Cutover (Lines 376-513 in migration.rs):**\n\n1. **Stop dual-write** - No new writes go to either node for affected shards\n2. **Drain phase** - Wait for all in-flight writes to complete (configurable timeout: 30s default)\n3. **Delta pass** - Re-read affected shards from OLD to catch stragglers written after last migration page\n4. **Activate** - Mark node active (routing switches to NEW-only)\n5. **Cleanup** - Delete migrated shard from OLD\n\n**Key Safety Mechanism (Lines 517-546):**\n```rust\nfn collect_delta_candidates(&self, id: MigrationId) \n    -> Result<HashMap<ShardId, Vec<String>>, MigrationError>\n```\n- Identifies writes that succeeded on OLD but failed on NEW\n- These are the exact documents that would be lost without delta pass\n\n---\n\n### **3. Anti-Entropy Reconciler (§13.8 / Phase 5)**\n\n#### **File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/anti_entropy.rs` (122 lines)\n\n**Key Types:**\n```rust\npub struct AntiEntropyConfig {\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub schedule_cron: String,        // Default: \"0 */6 * * *\" (every 6 hours)\n    pub shards_per_pass: u32,\n    pub max_read_concurrency: u32,    // Default: 2\n    pub fingerprint_batch_size: u32,  // Default: 1000\n    pub auto_repair: bool,\n    pub updated_at_field: String,     // \"_miroir_updated_at\"\n}\n```\n\n**Key Functions:**\n- `validate_migration_safety()`: **Hard refusal policy** - prevents unsafe cutover (Lines 43-51)\n- `migration_warning_if_ae_disabled()`: Warns when AE disabled during migration (Lines 59-69)\n\n**Safety Validation Logic:**\n```rust\npub fn validate_migration_safety(\n    ae_config: &AntiEntropyConfig,\n    migration_config: &MigrationConfig,\n) -> Result<(), MigrationError> {\n    if migration_config.skip_delta_pass && !ae_config.enabled {\n        return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n```\n\n**How Anti-Entropy Works (from plan docs):**\n1. **Fingerprint** - Paginated scan per shard using `filter=_miroir_shard={id}`\n2. **Diff** - Compare primary key sets and `_miroir_updated_at` timestamps\n3. **Repair** - For divergent docs, highest `_miroir_updated_at` wins\n4. **Origin tagging** - Repairs tagged with `_miroir_origin: antientropy` to suppress CDC\n\n---\n\n### **4. Migration Flow Overview**\n\n#### **Complete Migration Lifecycle:**\n\n```\n1. ComputingAssignments\n   └─> Determine which shards move to new node\n\n2. DualWriteMigrating  \n   └─> Start dual-write to both old and new nodes\n   └─> Background pagination copies docs from old→new\n   └─> Track in-flight writes\n\n3. CutoverBegin\n   └─> All shards complete background migration\n\n4. CutoverDraining\n   └─> Stop dual-write\n   └─> Wait for in-flight writes to settle\n   └─> Track completed/failed nodes per write\n\n5. CutoverDeltaPass (CRITICAL PHASE)\n   └─> Re-read source shards to catch stragglers\n   └─> Copy docs that succeeded on OLD but not NEW\n   └─> This closes the race window\n\n6. CutoverActivate\n   └─> Mark new node active\n   └─> Switch routing to NEW-only\n\n7. CutoverCleanup\n   └─> Delete migrated shard data from old nodes\n\n8. Complete\n```\n\n#### **The Race Window (Plan §15 OP#1):**\n\nThe dangerous window is between \"mark node active\" and \"delete migrated shard from old node.\" Documents written during dual-write that:\n1. Succeeded on OLD\n2. Failed on NEW\n3. Arrived after the last migration page\n\n**would be deleted from OLD without ever reaching NEW.**\n\n**Solution:** The delta pass (Phase 5) closes this window by ensuring NEW has a complete picture before committing the routing change.\n\n---\n\n### **5. Existing Test Infrastructure**\n\n#### **File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/tests/cutover_race.rs` (1774 lines)\n\n**Test Coverage (14 comprehensive chaos tests):**\n\n1. **`cutover_chaos_with_anti_entropy`** - AE on + delta on → 0 loss\n2. **`cutover_chaos_skip_delta_with_ae`** - AE on + delta skipped → measurable loss\n3. **`cutover_chaos_no_ae_with_delta`** - AE off + delta on → 0 loss\n4. **`cutover_chaos_no_ae_no_delta_blocked`** - Unsafe config refused\n5. **`cutover_chaos_boundary_burst`** - Writes at every phase transition\n6. **`cutover_chaos_high_volume`** - 100K writes, loss rate measurement\n7. **`cutover_chaos_loss_rate_no_ae_delta`** - 50K writes, AE off + delta on\n8. **`cutover_chaos_validation_gates`** - Unsafe config blocked at validation\n9. **`cutover_chaos_tight_loop_boundary`** - Rapid-fire writes at exact cutover\n10. **`cutover_chaos_loss_rate_1m_ae_on`** - 1M writes, AE on + delta on\n11. **`cutover_chaos_loss_rate_no_ae_no_delta`** - Quantify loss rate when unsafe\n12. **`cutover_chaos_concurrent_migration_writes`** - Writes during entire lifecycle\n13. **`cutover_chaos_three_node_cluster`** - 3-node cluster cutover\n14. **`cutover_chaos_three_node_no_ae_with_delta`** - 3-node, AE off + delta on\n\n**Test Infrastructure Components:**\n\n```rust\nstruct SimCluster {\n    data: HashMap<NodeId, HashMap<ShardId, HashSet<String>>>,\n}\n\nstruct RecordedWrite {\n    doc_id: String,\n    shard: ShardId,\n    succeeded_on_old: bool,\n    succeeded_on_new: bool,\n}\n```\n\n**Key Test Utilities:**\n- `dual_write()` - Simulates dual-write with configurable failure injection\n- `make_in_flight()` - Builds InFlightWrite from RecordedWrite\n- `run_delta_pass()` - Executes delta pass on simulated cluster\n- `lost_docs()` - Finds docs on old but not on new\n\n**Loss Rate Validation:**\n- Tests validate 0 loss with delta pass\n- Tests measure ~2% loss rate without delta pass (validates hard refusal policy)\n- Acceptance criterion: < 1 per 1M writes\n\n---\n\n### **6. Load Benchmarking**\n\n#### **File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/benches/reshard_load.rs` (237 lines)\n\n**Benchmark Matrix:**\n- Small docs (1KB) + moderate corpus (10GB)\n- Medium docs (10KB) + large corpus (100GB)\n- Large blobs (1MB) + very large corpus (1TB)\n\n**Validated Invariants (Plan §15 OP#3):**\n- Storage amplification = exactly 2.0×\n- Dual-write amplification = exactly 2.0×\n- Hash distribution CV < 5% (coefficient of variation)\n\n**Key Metrics:**\n- Peak storage during resharding\n- Peak write rate (dual-write + backfill)\n- Backfill duration\n- Per-node storage impact\n\n---\n\n### **7. CLI and Control Plane**\n\n#### **File:** `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-ctl/src/commands/reshard.rs` (283 lines)\n\n**CLI Commands:**\n```bash\nmiroir-ctl reshard start \\\n  --index products \\\n  --new-shards 256 \\\n  --throttle 10000 \\\n  --schedule-window off-peak \\\n  [--force] \\\n  [--dry-run]\n\nmiroir-ctl reshard status --index products\n```\n\n**Schedule Window Guard:**\n- Validates current time against allowed windows\n- Refuses to start outside windows unless `--force` is used\n- Configuration: `allowed_windows = [\"02:00-06:00\", \"22:00-23:30\"]`\n\n---\n\n### **8. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e174b7bffb5e411abc7db6bb","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Uses a simulated cluster + MigrationCoordinator to\n5\t//! stress-test every transition boundary.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! ## Variants\n8\t//!\n9\t//! - `cutover_chaos_with_anti_entropy`   — AE on, delta pass on → 0 loss\n10\t//! - `cutover_chaos_skip_delta_with_ae`  — AE on, delta skipped → measurable loss (AE repairs)\n11\t//! - `cutover_chaos_no_ae_with_delta`    — AE off, delta pass on → 0 loss\n12\t//! - `cutover_chaos_no_ae_no_delta_blocked` — unsafe config refused\n13\t//! - `cutover_chaos_boundary_burst`      — writes at every phase transition\n14\t//! - `cutover_chaos_high_volume`         — 100K writes, loss rate measurement\n15\t//! - `cutover_chaos_loss_rate_no_ae_delta` — loss rate with AE off + delta on\n16\t//! - `cutover_chaos_validation_gates`    — unsafe path blocked at config level\n17\t//! - `cutover_chaos_tight_loop_boundary` — rapid-fire writes at exact cutover instant\n18\t//! - `cutover_chaos_loss_rate_1m_ae_on`  — 1M writes, loss rate with AE on + delta\n19\t//! - `cutover_chaos_loss_rate_no_ae_no_delta` — AE off + delta off, quantify loss rate\n20\t//! - `cutover_chaos_concurrent_migration_writes` — writes during entire migration lifecycle\n21\t//! - `cutover_chaos_three_node_cluster` — 3-node cluster, writes at every transition boundary\n22\t//! - `cutover_chaos_three_node_no_ae_with_delta` — 3-node, AE off + delta on → 0 loss\n23\t\n24\tuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\n25\tuse std::time::Instant;\n26\t\n27\tuse miroir_core::migration::{\n28\t    InFlightWrite, MigrationConfig, MigrationCoordinator, MigrationError, MigrationPhase, NodeId,\n29\t    ShardId,\n30\t};\n31\t\n32\tfn node(s: &str) -> NodeId {\n33\t    NodeId(s.to_string())\n34\t}\n35\t\n36\tfn shard(id: u32) -> ShardId {\n37\t    ShardId(id)\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t/// Simulated cluster: tracks which documents exist on which node.\n41\tstruct SimCluster {\n42\t    data: HashMap<NodeId, HashMap<ShardId, HashSet<String>>>,\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\timpl SimCluster {\n46\t    fn new(nodes: &[NodeId]) -> Self {\n47\t        Self {\n48\t            data: nodes.iter().map(|n| (n.clone(), HashMap::new())).collect(),\n49\t        }\n50\t    }\n51\t\n52\t    fn put(&mut self, node: &NodeId, shard: ShardId, doc_id: &str) {\n53\t        self.data\n54\t            .entry(node.clone())\n55\t            .or_default()\n56\t            .entry(shard)\n57\t            .or_default()\n58\t            .insert(doc_id.to_string());\n59\t    }\n60\t\n61\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n62\t    fn all_docs_for_shards(&self, shards: &[ShardId]) -> HashSet<String> {\n63\t        let mut all = HashSet::new();\n64\t        for node_data in self.data.values() {\n65\t            for &s in shards {\n66\t                if let Some(docs) = node_data.get(&s) {\n67\t                    all.extend(docs.iter().cloned());\n68\t                }\n69\t            }\n70\t        }\n71\t        all\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Docs on old_node but NOT on new_node for given shards.\n75\t    fn lost_docs(&self, old_node: &NodeId, new_node: &NodeId, shards: &[ShardId]) -> Vec<String> {\n76\t        let mut lost = Vec::new();\n77\t        for &s in shards {\n78\t            let old_docs = self\n79\t                .data\n80\t                .get(old_node)\n81\t                .and_then(|m| m.get(&s))\n82\t                .cloned()\n83\t                .unwrap_or_default();\n84\t            let new_docs = self\n85\t                .data\n86\t                .get(new_node)\n87\t                .and_then(|m| m.get(&s))\n88\t                .cloned()\n89\t                .unwrap_or_default();\n90\t            for doc in &old_docs {\n91\t                if !new_docs.contains(doc.as_str()) {\n92\t                    lost.push(doc.clone());\n93\t                }\n94\t            }\n95\t        }\n96\t        lost\n97\t    }\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\tstruct RecordedWrite {\n101\t    doc_id: String,\n102\t    shard: ShardId,\n103\t    succeeded_on_old: bool,\n104\t    succeeded_on_new: bool,\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\t/// Simulate a dual-write. Returns what happened on each node.\n108\tfn dual_write(\n109\t    cluster: &mut SimCluster,\n110\t    old_node: &NodeId,\n111\t    new_node: &NodeId,\n112\t    shard: ShardId,\n113\t    doc_id: &str,\n114\t    old_fails: bool,\n115\t    new_fails: bool,\n116\t) -> RecordedWrite {\n117\t    if !old_fails {\n118\t        cluster.put(old_node, shard, doc_id);\n119\t    }\n120\t    if !new_fails {\n121\t        cluster.put(new_node, shard, doc_id);\n122\t    }\n123\t    RecordedWrite {\n124\t        doc_id: doc_id.to_string(),\n125\t        shard,\n126\t        succeeded_on_old: !old_fails,\n127\t        succeeded_on_new: !new_fails,\n128\t    }\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\t/// Build an InFlightWrite from a RecordedWrite.\n132\t/// Critically: if a node did NOT accept the write, mark it as *failed* (not\n133\t/// just missing from completed_nodes). This is what the drain logic expects —\n134\t/// `is_drained()` requires completed + failed == target count.\n135\tfn make_in_flight(w: &RecordedWrite, old_node: &NodeId, new_node: &NodeId) -> InFlightWrite {\n136\t    let mut completed = HashSet::new();\n137\t    let mut failed = HashMap::new();\n138\t\n139\t    if w.succeeded_on_old {\n140\t        completed.insert(old_node.clone());\n141\t    } else {\n142\t        failed.insert(old_node.clone(), \"write failed\".into());\n143\t    }\n144\t    if w.succeeded_on_new {\n145\t        completed.insert(new_node.clone());\n146\t    } else {\n147\t        failed.insert(new_node.clone(), \"write failed\".into());\n148\t    }\n149\t\n150\t    InFlightWrite {\n151\t        doc_id: w.doc_id.clone(),\n152\t        shard: w.shard,\n153\t        target_nodes: vec![old_node.clone(), new_node.clone()],\n154\t        completed_nodes: completed,\n155\t        failed_nodes: failed,\n156\t        submitted_at: Instant::now(),\n157\t    }\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\t/// Run a full delta pass on the simulated cluster: copy every doc on old but\n161\t/// not on new to new, then call shard_delta_complete for each shard.\n162\tfn run_delta_pass(\n163\t    coord: &mut MigrationCoordinator,\n164\t    cluster: &mut SimCluster,\n165\t    mid: miroir_core::migration::MigrationId,\n166\t    old_node: &NodeId,\n167\t    new_node: &NodeId,\n168\t    shards: &[ShardId],\n169\t) {\n170\t    for &s in shards {\n171\t        let lost: Vec<String> = cluster\n172\t            .data\n173\t            .get(old_node)\n174\t            .and_then(|m| m.get(&s))\n175\t            .map(|docs| {\n176\t                docs.iter()\n177\t m.get(&s))\n182\t                            .is_some_and(|nd| nd.contains(*d))\n183\t                    })\n184\t                    .cloned()\n185\t                    .collect()\n186\t            })\n187\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n188\t\n189\t        for doc_id in &lost {\n190\t            cluster.put(new_node, s, doc_id);\n191\t        }\n192\t        coord\n193\t            .shard_delta_complete(mid, s, lost.len() as u64)\n194\t            .unwrap();\n195\t    }\n196\t}\n197\t\n198\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n199\t// Test 1: AE on + delta pass on → 0 loss\n200\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n201\t\n202\t#[test]\n203\tfn cutover_chaos_with_anti_entropy() {\n204\t    let config = MigrationConfig {\n205\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n206\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n207\t        ..Default::default()\n208\t    };\n209\t    let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n210\t\n211\t    let old = node(\"old-0\");\n212\t    let new = node(\"new-3\");\n213\t    let shards = vec![shard(0), shard(1), shard(2)];\n214\t    let affected: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId> = shards.iter().map(|&s| (s, old.clone())).collect();\n215\t\n216\t    let mut cluster = SimCluster::new(&[old.clone(), new.clone()]);\n217\t\n218\t    // Pre-populate 1000 docs\n219\t    for i in 0..1000u64 {\n220\t        let s = shards[i as usize % shards.len()];\n221\t        cluster.put(&old, s, &format!(\"pre-{i}\"));\n222\t    }\n223\t\n224\t    // Migration\n225\t    let mid = coord.begin_migration(new.clone(), 0, affected).unwrap();\n226\t    coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n227\t\n228\t    // Dual-write phase: 2% failure on new\n229\t   let new_fails = i % 50 == 0;\n234\t        let w = dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, new_fails);\n235\t        boundary.push(w);\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    // Background migration done\n239\t    for &s in &shards {\n240\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, s, 500).unwrap();\n241\t    }\n242\t\n243\t    // Boundary writes (arrive between CutoverBegin and Verify there ARE lost docs before delta pass\n264\t    let lost_before = cluster.lost_docs(&old, &new, &shards);\n265\t    assert!(\n266\t        !lost_before.is_empty(),\n267\t        \"Expected some lost docs before delta pass\"\n268\t    );\n269\t\n270\t    // Delta pass repairs them\n271\t    run_delta_pass(&mut coord, &mut cluster, mid, &old, &new, &shards);\n272\t\n273\t    assert_eq!(\n274\t        coord.get_state(mid).unwrap().phase,\n275\t        MigrationPhase::CutoverCleanup\n276\t    );\n277\t    coord.complete_cleanup(mid).unwrap();\n278\t\n279\t    // Final: 0 loss\n280\t    let   );\n287\t\n288\t    let all = cluster.all_docs_for_shards(&shards);\n289\t    assert_eq!(all.len(), 2100, \"Expected 2100 docs, got {}\", all.len());\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n293\t// Test 2: AE on + delta skipped → measurable loss (AE would repair later)\n294\t// coord.begin_dual_write(mid).unwrap();\n319\t\n320\t    // Dual-write: 5% failure on new\n321\t    let mut expected_lost: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n322\t    let mut writes: Vec<RecordedWrite> =     let w = dual_write(&mut cluster, &old, &new, s, &doc_id, false, new_fails);\n328\t        if w.succeeded_on_old && !w.succeeded_on_new {\n329\t            expected_lost.push(doc_id);\n330\t        }\n331\t        writes.push(w);\n332\t    }\n333\t\n334\t    // Boundary writes — all succeed\n335\t    for i in 0..50u64 {\n336\t        let doc_id = format!(\"bnd-{i}\");\n337\t        let s = pre-existing docs (500) are on old only since background\n359\t    // migration copies them but our simulation doesn't track that copy. lost.len()\n379\t    );\n380\t}\n381\t\n382\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n383\t// Test 3: AE off + delta pass on → 0 loss (delta pass is sufficient
